Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore race in MemgraphDatabase async enumerables

Add FreshEnumerable<T> wrapper that calls the factory on every GetAsyncEnumerator()
call, ensuring each enumeration gets a brand-new async state machine instead of
cloning the original cached one.

Previously, async IAsyncEnumerable<T> state machines were created once and cached
inside Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<T>>. The C# runtime's GetAsyncEnumerator() clone
mechanism reuses internal fields (including ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore) in ways
that produce a race when the same IAsyncEnumerable is enumerated concurrently -
particularly via the .GetAwaiter().GetResult() path in SharpMUSHBooleanExpressionVisitor's
lock-expression trees, which blocks thread pool threads and amplifies contention.

Fixes all Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> and AsyncLazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> factories in
MapNodeToSharpObject, MapToSharpAttribute, and MapToLazySharpAttribute.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/023e7ce9-f225-4c2e-8d50-a902d9125d42

commit.message:
Two-period spending money with 3-day bill lead time

Overhaul useSpendingMoney to look across two pay periods:
- Period 1: checking balance minus bills due before next paycheck
- + Paycheck incoming
- Period 2: minus bills due before second paycheck
- Budget items prorated across the full window
- = Free Spending

Bills use a 3-day lead time for period slotting — a bill due on
the 1st is effectively slotted into the EOM pay period (due date
minus 3 days = 28th/29th). This ensures the mortgage and similar
bills are attributed to the paycheck that actually needs to cover
them, not the one after the due date.

Update SpendingMoneyBreakdown to show both periods separately
with individual bill line items, the incoming paycheck, and the
budget waterfall.

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

commit.message:
forecast: bypass KMeans init in GaussianHMM to fix MCP worker-thread hang

hmmlearn.GaussianHMM._init() runs sklearn KMeans to seed means, which
blocks indefinitely in asyncio.to_thread worker threads on Windows when
joblib probes CPU topology.  Pre-set percentile-based means so hmmlearn
skips the KMeans step -- identical pattern to the existing
_gaussian_mixture_init_kwargs workaround for GaussianMixture.

Fixes forecast_barrier_optimize timing out after 120s via MCP while
completing in ~3s via CLI.

commit.message:
fix: pre-fetch UTXO address before write lock to prevent spawn_blocking stall

Both the anchor check and old_addr_to_remove squatter-eviction check were calling
utxo_manager.get_utxo().await (which uses spawn_blocking internally) while holding
masternodes.write().await.  This caused tasks to queue on the write lock and starved
tokio workers, producing the RPC-timeout / watchdog-restart cycle seen on live nodes.

Fix: look up the UTXO address once before taking the write lock (prefetched_utxo_addr),
then reuse that value inside the lock for both checks.

commit.message:
feat: Timeseries Loader (#137)

# New Sub Command for Time Series

Takes in a ts group or tsid(s) and copies the data from one CDA instance
to another

`cwms-cli timeseries data -h`
gives
```bash
Usage: cwms-cli load timeseries data [OPTIONS]

  Copy timeseries data for a single location in a target CDA from a source CDA.

Options:
  -v, --verbose                   Increase verbosity (repeat for more detail).
  --dry-run / --no-dry-run        Show what would be written without storing to target.  [default: no-dry-run]
  --target-api-key TEXT           Target API key (if required by the target CDA).
  --target-cda TEXT               Target CWMS Data API root. Default: http://localhost:8081/cwms-data/  [required]
  --source-office TEXT            Source office ID (e.g. SWT, SWL).  [required]
  --source-cda TEXT               Source CWMS Data API root. Default: https://cwms-data.usace.army.mil/cwms-data/  [required]
  --ts-id TEXT                    ID of the timeseries to copy (e.g. 'LocID.*').
  --ts-group TEXT                 ID of the timeseries group to copy (e.g. 'GroupID.*').
  --ts-group-category-id TEXT     ID of the timeseries group category to copy.
  --ts-group-category-office-id TEXT
                                  ID of the timeseries group category office to copy.
  --begin [%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z|%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S %Z]
                                  Start date for timeseries data (e.g. '2022-01-01T00:00:00-06:00').
  --end [%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z|%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S %Z]
                                  End date for timeseries data (e.g. '2022-01-31T00:00:00-0600').
  -h, --help                      Show this message and exit.
```



This pull request introduces a new CLI command for copying timeseries
data between CWMS Data Access (CDA) instances, along with several
supporting improvements and fixes. The main focus is on enabling users
to copy timeseries data for a single location or a group of timeseries,
with robust input validation and dry-run support. Additional changes
include dependency updates and minor bug fixes.

**New timeseries data copy functionality:**

* Added a new `data` subcommand to the `timeseries` CLI group, allowing
users to copy timeseries data for a specific timeseries ID or a
timeseries group from a source CDA to a target CDA, with options for
specifying time range and group/category details. Input validation
ensures correct option usage. (`cwmscli/load/timeseries/timeseries.py`,
`cwmscli/load/timeseries/timeseries_data.py`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bd8fc5ed7f637c754f4f37d0841bdeb960ca74246d2889fdb801df2b29a8b66bR57-R147)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dbec006c6c9b478f5e30303a51327ea335a1bbf4d46355384afdc4b4f1b2c903R1-R109)

**Supporting improvements and fixes:**

* Updated the required `cwms-python` client library version from `0.8.0`
to `1.0.1` to ensure compatibility with new features.
(`cwmscli/utils/deps.py`)
* Fixed the order of `cwms.init_session` calls in `load_timeseries_ids`
to ensure the session is initialized for the target CDA before storing
timeseries data. (`cwmscli/load/timeseries/timeseries_ids.py`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-34fd913442e086124d05e5474a9842b5aecc6bcdfb014a2c89bdf19a6eccd0b1L30-L31)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-34fd913442e086124d05e5474a9842b5aecc6bcdfb014a2c89bdf19a6eccd0b1R40)
* Removed an unnecessary import of `pd` from the `turtle` module in
`timeseries_ids.py`. (`cwmscli/load/timeseries/timeseries_ids.py`)

**Minor CLI output adjustment:**

* Changed the `verbose` check for printing "Done." in the
`load_locations` command to always print the message, regardless of
verbosity. (`cwmscli/load/location/location_ids.py`)
